Title: Polymerization of proanthocyanidins under the catalysis of miR397a-regulated laccases in Salvia miltiorrhiza and Populus trichocarpa

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly/highly expressed in flowers; also functionally tested in transgenic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Proanthocyanidin (PA) biosynthesis/polymerization within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PA polymerization; B-type PA formation and B-type-to-A-type conversion
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (+)-catechin; (-)-epicatechin; procyanidin B1; procyanidin B2; procyanidin B3
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Procyanidin B1; procyanidin B2; procyanidin B3; procyanidin A1; procyanidin A2; other oxidized PA oligomers
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms polymerized PAs and converts B-type PAs to A-type PAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a main control step/main mechanism of PA formation in S. miltiorrhiza
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Proanthocyanidins (procyanidins A1, A2, B1, B2, B3)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense and stress protection; bioactive compounds with antioxidant, antibacterial, anti-diabetic, anti-viral, anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer activities; health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation and polymerization while reducing catechin and epicatechin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS/MS; 1H/13C NMR; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes flavan-3-ol monomers ((+)-catechin and (-)-epicatechin) into B-type procyanidins and catalyzes oxidative conversion of B-type procyanidins to A-type procyanidins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmLAC1 was the highest-expressed miR397a-targeted laccase in S. miltiorrhiza flowers and strongly downregulated in miR397a-overexpressing lines. 2. Recombinant SmLAC1 catalyzed in vitro polymerization of catechin/epicatechin to natural PA dimers and oligomers, including procyanidin B1, B2, B3, A1 and A2. 3. SmLAC1 also converted procyanidin B1/B2/B3 to A-type products. 4. Overexpression increased extractable/non-extractable PAs and procyanidins while decreasing catechin and epicatechin. 5. CRISPR knockout caused the opposite metabolite changes, confirming SmLAC1 as a core PA polymerization enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase PA accumulation and tune PA composition/polymerization degree in medicinal plants or woody crops; can be stacked with upstream flavan-3-ol biosynthetic genes, but elevated PAs may alter astringency or carbon allocation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased PA polymerization may reduce flavan-3-ol monomers and potentially affect sensory/astringency traits or metabolic flux; no obvious gross morphological phenotype reported for overexpression/knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays at acidic acetate buffer pH 5.0; greenhouse-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1210854; mass spectrometry data, Science Data Bank DOI 10.57760/sciencedb.19944
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both catechin and epicatechin can serve as starter and extension units during polymerization; enzyme showed high affinity to both substrates, with epicatechin reported as more favorable among monomers; among B-type dimers, procyanidin B2 was the more favorable substrate in one kinetic comparison

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flowers; downregulated in miR397a-overexpressing flowers
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Proanthocyanidin biosynthesis/polymerization within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely contributes to polymerized PA formation as part of miR397a-targeted LAC subset
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation in miR397a-overexpressing plants is associated with reduced polymerized PAs and increased catechin/epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in miR397a-overexpressing plants; association with PA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmLAC23 is a miR397a-targeted laccase downregulated in miR397a-overexpressing S. miltiorrhiza flowers. 2. Its reduction occurred together with lower total laccase activity, reduced PAs, and higher flavan-3-ol monomers. 3. Individual catalytic role was not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate auxiliary target for modulating PA polymerization together with SmLAC1 or miR397a
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core because explicitly quantified in Results as a miR397a-regulated LAC associated with PA changes, though not biochemically validated individually

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flowers; downregulated in miR397a-overexpressing flowers
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Proanthocyanidin biosynthesis/polymerization within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely contributes to polymerized PA formation as part of miR397a-targeted LAC subset
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation in miR397a-overexpressing plants is associated with reduced polymerized PAs and increased catechin/epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in miR397a-overexpressing plants; association with PA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmLAC24 is a miR397a-targeted laccase downregulated in transgenic flowers. 2. Downregulation coincided with reduced laccase activity and reduced PA accumulation. 3. Direct biochemical function was not individually tested.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate auxiliary target for PA engineering alongside SmLAC1/miR397a
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because specifically measured in Results as part of the miR397a-responsive PA-associated laccase subset

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flowers; downregulated in miR397a-overexpressing flowers
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Proanthocyanidin biosynthesis/polymerization within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely contributes to polymerized PA formation as part of miR397a-targeted LAC subset
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation in miR397a-overexpressing plants is associated with reduced polymerized PAs and increased catechin/epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in miR397a-overexpressing plants; association with PA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmLAC32 is a miR397a-targeted laccase downregulated in transgenic flowers. 2. Its reduction is associated with lowered total laccase activity and decreased PA accumulation. 3. Individual enzymatic role was not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate auxiliary target for PA engineering alongside SmLAC1/miR397a
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because specifically measured in Results as part of the miR397a-responsive PA-associated laccase subset

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Experimentally elevated in transgenic S. miltiorrhiza flowers and P. trichocarpa leaves
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Proanthocyanidin polymerization/biosynthesis via repression of laccases
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SmLAC1; SmLAC23; SmLAC24; SmLAC32; additional miR397-targeted SmLACs; PtLAC13; PtLAC37; PtLAC38; PtLAC39; PtLAC48 (proposed/orthologous targets)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates target laccase transcripts, causing strong downregulation and reduced total laccase activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: S. miltiorrhiza flowers; P. trichocarpa leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces polymerized PA accumulation and increases catechin and epicatechin monomers
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative master regulator of PA polymerization through coordinated repression of a subset of laccases in two plant species
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in S. miltiorrhiza and P. trichocarpa; qRT-PCR; laccase activity assay; metabolomics/UPLC; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of miR397a significantly downregulated multiple SmLAC genes and reduced total laccase activity in S. miltiorrhiza. 2. This caused severe reduction of extractable/non-extractable polymerized PAs and strong accumulation of catechin and epicatechin in both S. miltiorrhiza and P. trichocarpa. 3. Transcriptome data supported widespread downregulation of 18 flower-expressed miR397-targeted SmLACs. 4. The paper concludes that miR397a is a negative regulator of PA biosynthesis/polymerization.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful lever to decrease PA polymerization and increase flavan-3-ol monomers; however, because miR397 also targets lignification-related laccases, deployment should consider pleiotropic effects on lignin and development
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential reduction in lignin deposition and associated effects on structural traits, previously reported for miR397 in poplar/rice/Arabidopsis; in this study no obvious phenotype was observed in the tested lines
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1210854; metabolomics; mass spectrometry data, Science Data Bank DOI 10.57760/sciencedb.19944
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The mature S. miltiorrhiza miR397 sequence is identical to P. trichocarpa miR397a; the study used Ptr-MIR397a overexpression in both species
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible indirect feedback from flavan-3-ol accumulation on upstream phenylpropanoid/flavonoid genes, but not established as direct miR397a feedback
